description: This topic covers the responsibilities of the skipper and crew to protect the marine environment: pollution prevention (oil, garbage, sewage), environmentally responsible anchoring, and an awareness of the international framework (MARPOL) governing vessel discharges.
summary: This topic covers the responsibilities of the skipper and crew to protect the marine environment: pollution prevention (oil, garbage, sewage), environmentally responsible anchoring, and an awareness of the international framework (MARPOL) governing vessel discharges. The Day Skipper syllabus expects a working knowledge of what is and is not permitted to discharge at sea under UK/international law.

keyTerms.definition: International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships; governs vessel discharge rules
MARPOL Annex V — covers garbage disposal regulations
Bilge water contaminated with oil or fuel residue — must not be pumped overboard
Human waste — discharge regulations vary by distance from shore and jurisdiction
Onboard tank for retaining sewage until it can be properly discharged ashore or at sea in permitted areas

safetyNotes: Discharging plastics overboard is prohibited at all times and in all sea areas under MARPOL Annex V — this is an absolute rule with no distance-from-shore exception. MCQ candidates on this topic are marked [UNVERIFIED] pending primary source confirmation; do not include in assessments until verified against the MARPOL Annex V text.

Oily bilge water discharge overboard is prohibited under MARPOL Annex I for recreational craft. The correct procedure is retention onboard and disposal ashore at a licensed facility. As with plastics, the MCQ on this topic is marked [UNVERIFIED]; do not use in assessments until verified.

MARPOL Special Areas (e.g., North Sea, Baltic Sea, Mediterranean) impose stricter discharge restrictions than the general rules. This content is unverified from available transcripts and must not be included in assessment questions without primary source confirmation.

Violations of MARPOL or domestic pollution regulations can result in criminal prosecution and significant fines. Instructors should present these as legal obligations, not just best-practice guidance.

UK domestic sewage discharge regulations for pleasure vessels (Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Pollution by Sewage and Garbage from Ships) Regulations 2020) are unverified from available transcripts — see Unverified / Needs Citation section.

flashcards.front: What international convention governs pollution prevention from vessels?
flashcards.back: MARPOL — the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships

flashcards.front: Which MARPOL Annex covers garbage disposal from vessels?
flashcards.back: Annex V

flashcards.front: May oily bilge water be pumped overboard?
flashcards.back: No — it must not be discharged overboard; it must be retained and disposed of ashore or via a licensed waste facility

flashcards.front: What environmental consideration should guide anchor site selection?
flashcards.back: Avoid anchoring on sensitive or protected seabed habitats; prefer sand or mud over reefs

flashcards.front: What is a holding tank used for?
flashcards.back: Retaining sewage onboard for disposal in permitted areas or ashore, rather than discharging it directly into the sea

mcqQuestions.question: Q: Which MARPOL Annex specifically governs the disposal of garbage (including plastics) from vessels?
When is it generally prohibited to discharge plastics overboard from a recreational vessel?
options: Only within 3 nautical miles of land
At all times, regardless of location

mcqQuestions.question: From an environmental standpoint, which seabed type is preferable for anchoring?
What should a skipper do with oily bilge water accumulated in the bilge?
options: Pump it overboard if more than 3nm offshore
Dilute it with fresh water before pumping
Retain it and dispose of it ashore at a proper facility
Filter it through an oily water separator and pump overboard at any location
